title: Rates, Ratios, and Units
skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units

questions.prompt: The International Space Station orbits Earth at an average speed of 4.76 miles per second. What is the station’s average speed in miles per hour?
questions.rationale: Convert seconds to hours: 1 hour = 3,600 seconds. Speed = 4.76 × 3,600 = 17,136 miles per hour.

questions.prompt: Based on the table, what is the ratio of the cost per gram of protein in a large egg to the cost per gram of protein in a cup of milk?
How many tablespoons are equivalent to 14 teaspoons? (3 teaspoons = 1 tablespoon)
A distance of 112 furlongs is equivalent to how many feet? (1 furlong = 220 yards and 1 yard = 3 feet)
Paul Revere rode 11 miles in 26 minutes. What was his average speed, to the nearest tenth of a mile per hour?
The equation d = 55t gives the distance d (in miles) traveled in t hours. How many times the distance traveled after 3k hours is the distance traveled after k hours?
questions.rationale: Distance is proportional to time. Tripling time triples distance.

questions.prompt: A park has an area of 11,863,808 square yards. What is the area, in square miles? (1 mile = 1,760 yards)
An object has a mass of 168 grams and a volume of 24 cubic centimeters. What is the density, in grams per cubic centimeter?
Based on the table, what is closest to the difference, in inches, between the diameters of a white birch and a pin oak after 10 years?
questions.rationale: Using growth factors, the diameter difference after 10 years is approximately 1.3 inches.

questions.prompt: For values j and k, the ratio j to k is 11 to 12. If j is multiplied by 17, what must k be multiplied by to maintain the same ratio?
questions.rationale: To preserve a ratio, both quantities must be multiplied by the same factor.
